{"id":"rc-wwh.1","title":"T1: Project scaffolding & shared interfaces","description":"Create the project foundation. Set up pyproject.toml with deps (torch, transformers, reservoirpy, peft, wandb, scipy, ruff, mypy, pytest). Create src/ package layout per IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Section 7: src/reservoir/, src/models/, src/training/, src/eval/, src/eval/benchmarks/, src/data/. Create tests/ dir. Set up pre-commit with ruff+mypy. Add CI skeleton (.github/workflows/).

Define shared protocols and types in src/types.py (or src/types/):
- ReservoirConfig dataclass (size, spectral_radius, leak_rate, input_scaling, topology)
- Reservoir protocol (step/reset methods)
- ModelWrapper protocol (forward/generate/get_hidden)
- BenchmarkExample dataclass (input/target/metadata)
- EvalResult dataclass (task/metric/value/config)
- Generator protocol for benchmarks
- DataPipeline protocol (iterate/split)

Acceptance: pip install -e . works, pytest runs (even if no tests yet), linter passes, all protocols importable from src.types.

CPU only. No dependencies.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:33:54Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:33:54Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.1","title":"T1: Project scaffolding & shared interfaces","description":"Create the project foundation. Set up pyproject.toml with deps (torch, transformers, reservoirpy, peft, wandb, scipy, ruff, mypy, pytest). Create src/ package layout per IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Section 7: src/reservoir/, src/models/, src/training/, src/eval/, src/eval/benchmarks/, src/data/. Create tests/ dir. Set up pre-commit with ruff+mypy. Add CI skeleton (.github/workflows/).

Define shared protocols and types in src/types.py (or src/types/):
- ReservoirConfig dataclass (size, spectral_radius, leak_rate, input_scaling, topology)
- Reservoir protocol (step/reset methods)
- ModelWrapper protocol (forward/generate/get_hidden)
- BenchmarkExample dataclass (input/target/metadata)
- EvalResult dataclass (task/metric/value/config)
- Generator protocol for benchmarks
- DataPipeline protocol (iterate/split)

Acceptance: pip install -e . works, pytest runs (even if no tests yet), linter passes, all protocols importable from src.types.

CPU only. No dependencies.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:33:54Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:42:54Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.2","title":"T2: ESN reservoir core library","description":"Implement src/reservoir/esn.py — ESN class with configurable size (500–50K nodes), spectral radius, leak rate, input scaling, sparse topology (Erdős–Rényi, small-world). Core method: step(x_t, w_t) → r_t for state evolution. Use sparse matrix storage (scipy.sparse or torch.sparse). Support batched forward for GPU tensors.

Key implementation details:
- Initialize weight matrix W with desired spectral radius (generate random sparse, rescale eigenvalues)
- Input weight matrix W_in: dense or sparse, configurable scaling
- State update: r_t = (1-α)*r_{t-1} + α*tanh(W*r_{t-1} + W_in*x_t + w_t)
- α = leak rate
- Implement both Erdős–Rényi (random edges with probability p) and small-world (Watts-Strogatz) topologies
- reset() method to zero state

Tests in tests/test_esn.py:
- Echo state property: spectral radius < 1 ⟹ state fading memory
- Performance: <1 ms per step at 50K nodes on CPU
- State norm bounded over 10K steps across parameter regimes

Depends on: T1 (for types/protocols). CPU only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:34:05Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:34:05Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.4","title":"T4: Evaluation harness","description":"Implement src/eval/harness.py — run any benchmark suite against any model, output standardized JSON.

Features:
- evaluate(model: ModelWrapper, benchmarks: list[Generator], config: EvalConfig) → list[EvalResult]
- Support few-shot prompting (0-shot, k-shot with examples)
- Greedy and sampling decode modes
- Per-task metrics: exact match, perplexity, accuracy, F1
- Batch evaluation for throughput (configurable batch size)
- JSON output format with full reproducibility info (model name, config, timestamp, git hash)
- Progress tracking (tqdm or similar)
- Resume from partial results (append mode)

The harness should be model-agnostic — works with any ModelWrapper. Benchmarks provide (input, target, metadata) via the Generator protocol from src/types.py.

Tests in tests/test_harness.py:
- Harness runs a dummy benchmark against a dummy model
- Produces valid JSON results matching EvalResult schema
- Metrics computed correctly on known inputs

Depends on: T1 (for protocols). CPU only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:34:25Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:34:25Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.3","title":"T3: Model loading & tokenizer utilities","description":"Implement src/models/loader.py — unified interface to load models in FP16/BF16:
- Qwen3.5-0.8B-Base (hybrid DeltaNet + full-attention architecture)
- LLaMA-3.2-1B (pure softmax attention)
- Mamba-2 1.3B (SSM architecture)

Features:
- load_model(model_name, dtype, device) → ModelWrapper
- Tokenizer wrappers with consistent API across model families
- Layer-name inspection utilities: list all layer names, identify DeltaNet vs full-attention layers in Qwen3.5, identify LoRA target modules
- get_hidden_states(model, input_ids, layer_indices) utility
- VRAM estimation before loading

The ModelWrapper should implement the protocol from src/types.py (forward/generate/get_hidden).

Acceptance: All three model families load on GPU, forward pass produces logits of correct shape, Qwen3.5 DeltaNet layers identified by name. Add tests in tests/test_loader.py (can be smoke tests that mock model loading for CI).

Depends on: T1 (for ModelWrapper protocol). CPU only (downloads models, no training). Brief GPU smoke test.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:34:16Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:34:16Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.5","title":"T5: Synthetic benchmark suite","description":"Implement benchmark generators in src/eval/benchmarks/:

src/eval/benchmarks/memory.py:
- Passkey retrieval: hide a passkey at configurable depth in distractor text, query at end. Configurable: context length, passkey position, distractor type.
- Variable tracking: define variables, perform assignments across distractors, query final value. Configurable: num variables, num operations, distractor length.
- Associative recall: present key-value pairs, add distractor, query by key. Configurable: num pairs, delay length.

src/eval/benchmarks/computation.py:
- Multi-digit addition/multiplication with carry (configurable digit count)
- Modular arithmetic (a op b mod m, configurable operand size and modulus)
- Dyck language recognition (balanced parentheses, configurable depth and length)
- Synthetic program execution traces (simple variable assignments and arithmetic)

src/eval/benchmarks/emergent.py:
- Compositional generalization: train on subset of operators, test on held-out combinations
- Length extrapolation: generate at train length, test at 2x-10x length
- Algorithmic transfer: train on one algorithm, test on related algorithm

All generators produce BenchmarkExample(input, target, metadata) tuples with configurable difficulty. Each generator must produce 1K+ valid examples. Format must be compatible with T4 harness (Generator protocol from src/types.py).

Tests in tests/test_benchmarks.py. Depends on: T1. CPU only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:34:36Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:34:36Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.6","title":"T6: Chaotic systems data pipeline","description":"Implement src/data/chaos.py — generate trajectories for chaotic dynamical systems.

Systems to implement:
1. Lorenz-63: dx/dt = σ(y-x), dy/dt = x(ρ-z)-y, dz/dt = xy-βz. Standard params: σ=10, ρ=28, β=8/3.
2. Mackey-Glass: dx/dt = β*x(t-τ)/(1+x(t-τ)^n) - γ*x(t). Variable τ for different chaos levels.
3. Kuramoto-Sivashinsky: u_t = -u*u_x - u_xx - u_xxxx. PDE, discretize spatially.

Features:
- generate_trajectory(system, params, T, dt, transient) → array
- Train/val/test splits (temporal, non-overlapping)
- Normalization (per-variable z-score)
- Lyapunov time computation for evaluation horizon calibration
- DataLoader-compatible output

Validation:
- Lorenz-63 max Lyapunov exponent λ ≈ 0.906 (verify numerically)
- Mackey-Glass transitions to chaos at τ ≈ 17
- KS spatial statistics match known results

Tests in tests/test_chaos.py:
- Generated trajectories match known Lyapunov exponents
- Splits are non-overlapping
- Data loads in <1s

Depends on: T1. CPU only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:34:46Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:34:46Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.25","title":"T25: Training curriculum & data pipeline","description":"Implement src/training/curriculum.py — the three-stage training curriculum for Track C pre-training.

Stage 1: Standard next-token prediction
- General text from FineWeb/RedPajama
- Standard causal LM objective
- Context length: 4K tokens

Stage 2: Procedural objective mixing
- Mix general text with procedural tasks:
  - Arithmetic traces (from T5 computation benchmarks)
  - Symbolic state tracking
  - Dyck language sequences
- Dynamic mixing ratios (configurable, e.g., 70% text / 30% procedural)
- Context length: 4K tokens

Stage 3: Length curriculum
- Gradually increase context length: 4K → 8K → 16K → 32K → 128K
- Maintain procedural mix from Stage 2
- Optional: auxiliary reservoir-state prediction loss (predict next reservoir state as regularizer)

Implementation:
- CurriculumDataPipeline class implementing DataPipeline protocol
- Streaming data loading (datasets library with streaming=True for large corpus)
- Dynamic mixing ratios per stage
- Automatic stage transitions based on step count or loss plateau
- Support for resuming from checkpoint at any stage

Tests in tests/test_curriculum.py:
- Data pipeline produces batches at each curriculum stage
- Mixing ratios are correct
- Streaming works (doesn't load full corpus into memory)
- Stage transitions work correctly

Depends on: T5 (benchmark generators for procedural data). CPU only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:37:46Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:37:46Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.10","title":"T10: LLaMA-3.2-1B pure-attention baseline","description":"Evaluate LLaMA-3.2-1B (pure softmax attention, no DeltaNet) on the full benchmark suite. Architecture control for the DeltaNet synergy hypothesis — needed to compare against T17 (LLaMA + reservoir).

Script: scripts/eval_llama.py

Evaluation:
- Full benchmark suite from T5
- Same metrics as T7 for direct comparison

Output: results/baselines/llama32_1b.json

Acceptance: Full results JSON, direct comparison vs T7.

Depends on: T3 (model loading), T4 (eval harness), T5 (benchmarks).
GPU task, ~3 hours.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:35:13Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:35:13Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.13","title":"T13: LoRA + adapter training pipeline","description":"Implement src/training/lora_trainer.py — training loop for frozen LLM + LoRA + reservoir interface.

Features:
- LoRATrainer class with train(model, reservoir, interface, dataset, config) method
- Freeze base model, only train: LoRA params + interface params (ReadProjection, WriteHead, CrossAttentionSidecar)
- AdamW optimizer with separate learning rates for LoRA vs interface params
- Gradient checkpointing for VRAM efficiency
- Mixed precision (BF16) training
- Wandb logging (loss, lr, grad norms, reservoir state stats)
- Configurable: LoRA rank (4-64), target modules, learning rate schedule (cosine with warmup), dataset mixing ratios
- Checkpoint save/load (save LoRA + interface weights, not frozen model)
- Early stopping based on validation loss
- Data loading: support for multiple datasets with mixing ratios

Config: configs/lora_training.yaml template

Tests in tests/test_trainer.py:
- Pipeline runs a 10-step smoke test without OOM (can use small dummy model)
- Loss decreases over steps
- Checkpoints save and load correctly
- Only expected params have gradients

Depends on: T3 (model loading). CPU only (builds pipeline, training tested on GPU in smoke test).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:35:43Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:35:43Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.11","title":"T11: Infini-attention / Titans baseline","description":"Implement compressive memory baseline on Qwen3.5-0.8B-Base. This is Control 3 — the strongest non-reservoir memory baseline. Choose one of:
- Infini-attention (compressive memory with linear attention for long-range)
- Titans/ATLAS-style test-time memory module

Implementation in src/models/infini_attention.py or src/models/titans_memory.py:
- Add compressive memory module to selected layers of Qwen3.5
- Train adapter + memory module using same fine-tuning data as Track A will use
- Use LoRA for the base model params

Training:
- Same fine-tuning mix that Track A will use
- LoRA + memory module parameters
- ~5K training steps

Evaluation:
- Full benchmark suite, especially memory benchmarks
- Compare vs T7 vanilla baseline

Output: results/baselines/infini_attention.json (or titans.json)

Acceptance: Trained model exists, eval results on memory benchmarks recorded, comparison vs T7 documented.

Depends on: T3 (model loading), T4 (eval harness), T5 (benchmarks).
GPU task, ~15 hours (implementation + training + eval).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:35:22Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:35:22Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.9","title":"T9: Mamba-2 1.3B native SSM baseline","description":"Evaluate Mamba-2 (1.3B) on the full benchmark suite. This is Control 2 — tests whether SSM architectures natively solve the memory gap without reservoir augmentation.

Script: scripts/eval_mamba2.py

Evaluation:
- Full benchmark suite from T5 (memory, computation, emergent)
- Same metrics as T7 for direct comparison
- Throughput and VRAM comparison

Output: results/baselines/mamba2_1.3b.json

Acceptance: Full results JSON, direct comparison table vs T7 (Qwen3.5 vanilla).

Depends on: T3 (model loading — Mamba-2 support), T4 (eval harness), T5 (benchmarks).
GPU task, ~5 hours.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:35:09Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:35:09Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.8","title":"T8: Qwen3.5-0.8B + RoPE/YaRN context extension","description":"Apply YaRN-style RoPE scaling to extend Qwen3.5-0.8B-Base context window. This is Control 1 from the proposal — tests whether simple positional extension solves the memory gap.

Implementation:
- Apply YaRN (Yet another RoPE extensioN) scaling to RoPE embeddings
- Use transformers library's rope_scaling config if available, or implement manually
- Scale factor to extend from native context to 128K+

Evaluation:
- Long-context benchmarks: passkey retrieval at 32K, 64K, 128K+ positions
- Variable tracking at long range
- Compare directly against T7 vanilla baselines

Output: results/baselines/qwen35_yarn.json

Acceptance: Long-context eval results at 32K, 64K, 128K+ positions recorded. Direct comparison table vs T7.

Depends on: T3 (model loading), T4 (eval harness), T5 (benchmarks).
GPU task, ~3 hours.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mcosma@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:35:04Z","created_by":"wakamex","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:35:04Z"}

{"id":"rc-wwh.7","title":"T7: Qwen3.5-0.8B-Base vanilla baselines","description":"Evaluate unmodified Qwen3.5-0.8B-Base on the full benchmark suite (T5 benchmarks + T6 chaotic systems). This is the PRIMARY reference point for all subsequent comparisons.

Script: scripts/run_baselines.py or scripts/eval_qwen_vanilla.py

Record:
- Perplexity on held-out text
- Accuracy on all synthetic tasks (memory, computation, emergent benchmarks from T5)
- Chaotic prediction quality (trajectory prediction vs Lyapunov time from T6)
- Throughput (tokens/sec)
- VRAM usage
- Inference latency (p50, p95)

Output: results/baselines/qwen35_vanilla.json (standardized EvalResult format from T4 harness)

Acceptance: Complete results JSON for ALL benchmark categories, baseline numbers recorded and reproducible.

Depends on: T3 (model loading), T4 (eval harness), T5 (benchmarks), T6 (chaos data).
